model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Duke enters as the consensus #1 team in the country by both KenPom (+39.17 AdjEM) and Torvik, boasting the nation's top-ranked defense and a 31-2 record built against elite ACC competition. Siena, a MAAC automatic qualifier ranked 192nd by KenPom with a negative AdjEM of -2.11, presents one of the weakest 16-seed profiles in recent memory — their offense ranks 207th nationally and their defense 175th, creating a 41-point efficiency gap that is historically insurmountable. The betting market opened Duke as a 29-point favorite, one of the largest spreads in tournament history, and positive injury news with Patrick Ngongba II expected to return means Duke enters near full strength. No credible upset path exists for Siena against this caliber of opponent, and the historical 1-vs-16 record (160-1 in the modern era) underscores the near-certainty of a Duke victory.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs. Siena — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is the overwhelming favorite in every measurable dimension: efficiency ratings, schedule strength, injury status, and betting market consensus. Siena's MAAC championship is a feel-good story but provides no statistical basis for an upset against the nation's #1 team.
evidence: KenPom: Duke #1 (AdjEM +39.17, AdjO 127.9 ranked 4th, AdjD 88.7 ranked 1st) vs. Siena #192 (AdjEM -2.11, AdjO 107.2 ranked 207th, AdjD 109.3) — a 41.28-point efficiency gap.
Torvik: Duke #1 (AdjOE 128.0 ranked 4th, AdjDE 90.4 ranked 1st) vs. Siena #187 (AdjOE 107.2 ranked 197th, AdjDE 108.9 ranked 175th).
Betting market: Duke opened as a 29-point favorite over Siena, among the largest spreads in NCAA Tournament Round of 64 history; total set at 136 points.
Injury news: Duke's Patrick Ngongba II (foot) expected to return for the tournament; Caleb Foster (fractured foot) also hoping to play, giving Duke near-full roster availability.
Siena context: Won 2026 MAAC Championship 64-54 over Merrimack for their first NCAA bid since 2010; finished 23-11 overall in a weak mid-major conference.
Historical 1-vs-16 record: 160-1 in the modern era (only UMBC in 2018); Siena's negative AdjEM and below-average defense offer no credible upset indicators.

result.title: Siena Wins 2026 Air Force Reserve MAAC Men's Basketball Championship ...
result.snippet: The Saints have now won seven MAAC trophies, their first title since 2020, which is the second-most MAAC Championships in league history. 2026 marks the first completed MAAC tournament title for Siena since winning the 2010 crown and this will also be the first NCAA tournament appearance for the Saints since 2010.

result.title: 2026 MAAC men's basketball tournament - Wikipedia
result.snippet: The tournament was played March 5-10, 2026 , at the Jim Whelan Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey. [1][2] The tournament winner, Siena , received the conference's automatic bid to the 2026 NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament . This was the last conference tournament under the Metro Atlantic Athletic Conference name.

result.title: Seventh Heaven | Siena University
result.snippet: Awarded the 2020 MAAC Championship and NCAA Automatic Qualifier as the highest remaining seed when COVID hit, the Saints will finally get to actually play in the NCAA Tournament for the first time in 16 years after winning the program's first MAAC Championship Game since 2010.

result.title: Siena triumphs in MAAC title game to secure NCAA tournament berth
result.snippet: Coach Gerry McNamara has guided Siena to its first NCAA tournament berth since 2010 following Tuesday night's 64-54 win over Merrimack in the MAAC championship game.
